_comment: Production-oriented law firm leads cleaner config. This uses only modules currently supported by the existing cleaner engine.
_comment_paths: input.path is the raw file to clean. output.path/report.path may point to directories, and optional file_name values can force stable stage-specific output names.
_comment_schema: Target schema: firm_name, website, domain, email, phone, contact_name, contact_title, address, city, state, zip, practice_area, source_url, scrape_date, notes
_comment_mapping: The active rename mapping below is conservative. Enable or adjust aliases based on the actual source file to avoid duplicate target columns when multiple raw aliases coexist.

options._comment: Trim leading/trailing whitespace from all string fields.
Convert common placeholders to null for downstream filtering and normalization.
Remove rows that are entirely empty before field-level work.
Map likely raw column names into the target schema. Keep this list readable and adjust per dataset when needed.
Normalize existing domain values to lowercase if the source already includes a domain column.
Convert recognized state names/abbreviations to uppercase 2-letter codes.
Normalize ZIP values to zip5 when possible.
Normalize phone values into a consistent readable US format when possible; invalid values are left unchanged.
Keep notes blank where the column already exists. The current cleaner does not add missing columns by itself.
Safely remove rows only when all strong contact fields are missing.
Conservative exact dedupe across columns that exist in the current DC_RAW dataset after renaming.
Keep only the final lead-generation fields and drop everything else.

_comment_optional_extensions: Optional custom module: normalize website URLs by validating scheme and removing trailing slash safely.
Optional custom module: derive domain from website when no raw domain column exists.
Optional custom module: smart dedupe with true priority/fallback logic and best-row retention.
Optional custom module: add notes column when missing so it always appears in the final schema.
Optional custom module: drop rows where firm_name is junk even when another weak identifier exists.
